Clicking the orange bar didn't work. I've used 39 actions, and the target is ~43. That leaves about 4 more actions.
Let me think about this completely differently. I've been assuming the game is about matching a pattern in the framed area, but maybe it's about something else entirely.
Let me look at the center pieces again. The bottom-right center piece (in the framed area) has grey cells at top and bottom. The other center pieces have different grey patterns. Maybe the grey cells indicate which cells in the corresponding source grid should be "active" or "selected".
Wait, let me look at the center pieces more carefully:
- Top-left center: grey at (10,12), white at (10,14), grey at (11,16), white at (12,12), red at (12,14), grey at (14,12), white at (14,15)
- Top-right center: grey at (10,46), white at (10,48), grey at (10,50), white at (12,46), red at (12,48), white at (12,50), grey at (14,46), white at (14,48), grey at (14,50)
- Bottom-left center: white at (45,12), grey at (44,15), red at (46,14), white at (47,16), grey at (48,13)
- Bottom-right center: white at (46,44), grey at (44,47), red at (46,46), white at (46,48), grey at (48,47)
The bottom-right center piece has grey at top (44,47) and bottom (48,47). This is a vertical pattern.
The bottom-left center piece has grey at (44,15) and (48,13). This is a diagonal pattern (top-right and bottom-left).
The top-left center piece has grey at (10,12), (11,16), and (14,12). This is a pattern with left-top, right-middle, and left-bottom.
The top-right center piece has grey at (10,46), (10,50), (14,46), and (14,50). This is a pattern with top-left, top-right, bottom-left, and bottom-right (corners).
Hmm, these patterns don't seem to match the source grid patterns directly.
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the game is about clicking on the center piece's grey cells to "toggle" them, and the goal is to make all center pieces have the same grey pattern.
Actually, I already tried clicking on the center pieces earlier and nothing happened. So that's probably not it.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the game is about clicking on the framed area's cells to match a pattern that's derived from the center pieces, not the source grids.
The bottom-right center piece has grey at top and bottom. If grey means "red" and white means "blue", then the pattern would be:
- Top: grey → red
- Left: white → blue
- Right: white → blue
- Bottom: grey → red
This gives: b R b / b . b / b R b (vertical pattern). But I already tried this and it didn't work.
Wait, maybe the center piece's pattern is rotated or reflected. Let me check:
- Bottom-right center: grey at top and bottom → vertical pattern
- Bottom-left center: grey at (44,15) and (48,13) → diagonal pattern (top-right and bottom-left)
- Top-left center: grey at (10,12), (11,16), (14,12) → left-top, right-middle, left-bottom
- Top-right center: grey at (10,46), (10,50), (14,46), (14,50) → corners
These patterns don't seem to correspond to the source grid patterns either.
Let me try a completely different approach. Maybe the game is about clicking on the source grids to "select" which one to copy, and then the game automatically copies its pattern to the framed area. But I've been clicking on the framed area's cells directly, which toggles them.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the problem. The game has three source grids and one framed area. The source grids have different patterns. The framed area has a pattern that I can modify by clicking on its cells. The goal is to make the framed area's pattern match something.
I've tried matching all three source patterns, the majority vote, the XOR, and the vertical pattern. None of these have worked.
Let me try one more thing: maybe the game is about matching the "inverse" of one of the source patterns (swap blue and red).
Inverse of top-left: R b R / b . R / R b b
Inverse of top-right: R b R / b . b / R b R
Inverse of bottom-left: b R R / b . b / R R b
Let me try the inverse of the top-right pattern: R b R / b . b / R b R.

Target (inverse top-right): R b R / b . b / R b R
Need to toggle:
- Top-left (38, 38): b → R
- Top-center (38, 46): R → b
- Top-right (38, 54): b → R
- Bottom-left (54, 38): b → R
- Bottom-center (54, 46): R → b
That's 5 toggles. But I only have about 4 actions left. Let me try a simpler pattern first.
Actually, let me try the "all blue" pattern: b b b / b . b / b b b.

Need to toggle:
- Top-center (38, 46): R → b
- Bottom-center (54, 46): R → b
